◈ epost_agent_kit
Agents CLI Workflows Install GitHub
All Workflows
Maintenance Automation

Cross-Branch Fix Propagation

Fix a bug once — agents find the same pattern in other branches and propagate the fix with context-aware patches and automated verification.

epost-debugger epost-code-reviewer epost-fullstack-developer epost-tester epost-git-manager

How It Works

1

Detect

epost-debugger

A fix is committed on one branch. The agent extracts the pattern: what changed, why, and what the buggy code looked like.

2

Scan

epost-code-reviewer

Search other active branches for the same vulnerable pattern. Grep across branches, match by AST structure — not just text.

3

Patch

epost-fullstack-developer

For each affected branch, generate a context-aware patch. The fix adapts to local changes — no blind cherry-pick.

4

Verify

epost-tester

Run tests on each patched branch. If tests pass, open a PR. If they fail, flag for human review with context.

5

Notify

epost-git-manager

Open PRs on affected branches with a cross-reference to the original fix. Branch owners get context, not surprises.

Real-World Examples

Session token refresh race condition

Fixed on feat/auth-redesign
Pattern Concurrent token refresh without mutex
Also affected
feat/notificationsfeat/settings-v2release/3.2

SQL injection in search query builder

Fixed on hotfix/search-injection
Pattern String interpolation in SQL WHERE clause
Also affected
feat/advanced-searchfeat/analytics-export

Missing null check on API response

Fixed on fix/inbox-crash
Pattern response.data.items access without optional chaining
Also affected
feat/inbox-redesignfeat/smart-send-v2
⇄

Not Cherry-Pick. Smart Propagation.

Cherry-picking fails when branches have diverged. This workflow understands the intent of the fix — what the bug was and why the new code is correct — then generates a context-aware patch for each branch's current state. Conflicts are resolved by the agent, not by you.

◈ epost_agent_kit v2.0.0
Works with any AI coding assistant
